prompt: If my sleep schedule is sporadic, what should I do for body composition, recovery, and training? Are naps useful, and should I ever train sleep deprived?
expected_output: They said poor sleep often worsens body composition via appetite/self-control effects, so controlling food environment becomes extra important when sleep deprived. For training, they advised keeping resistance training in place but biasing toward safer options (machines, dumbbells, controlled bodybuilding-style work) when motor control is compromised. They cautioned against high-risk sessions like max free-weight attempts or hard heat-exposed conditioning when severely sleep deprived. On naps, they said short naps around 20-30 minutes are often useful, while some people tolerate longer ~90-minute naps if nighttime sleep is not disrupted. Overall guidance: keep training, prioritize safety, and manage sleep-driven behavior drift rather than abandoning training.
assertions: Answer recommends controlling food environment when sleep deprived due to appetite effects
Answer suggests biasing toward safer training options (machines, controlled movements) when fatigued
Answer mentions short naps of 20-30 minutes as useful
Answer advises keeping resistance training in place but with safety adjustments
